summary: sensivity ships obfuscated payload with PowerShell/VBScript launcher
details: The package contains a launcher.js that uses Node's child_process module to invoke PowerShell (execSync('powershell...'), spawn('powershell',...)) and to execute a.vbs script via wscript at lines 14-27. It also ships server.obf.js, a heavily obfuscated single-line bundle using hex-mangled identifiers (_0x4ac1e2, _0x47a9eb, _0x492314, etc.) — a deliberate effort to hide the payload from review. The combination of a Windows-shell + VBScript launcher with an obfuscated server payload in a small npm package has no legitimate library use case and matches the shape of a Windows-targeted dropper/RAT. Installing or loading this package gives the publisher arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine via the obfuscated payload, executed through the PowerShell/wscript launchers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
